senses.definition: having planks or boards arranged to create a flat surface on a ship's hull or deck, typically used as a nautical descriptor
examples: The shiplapped hull gave the ship a smooth exterior.
They repaired the shiplapped sides after the voyage.

senses.definition: the act or process of applying ship planks to a hull or deck; building a ship with planked exterior
examples: Shiplapping was completed by skilled carpenters.
The shipyard specializes in shiplapping techniques.

senses.definition: wooden boards or planks used in ship construction to cover the hull or deck
examples: The shipyard stored extra shiplaps for repairs.
They replaced several worn shiplaps along the bow.

senses.definition: without a ship or lacking a means to transport goods by sea
examples: The coast was shipless after the port closed.
A shipless fleet could not compete overseas.

senses.definition: in a shipless or unseaworthy manner; lacking a ship or means of transport
examples: The fleet sailed shiplessly after the loss of the ships.
The cargo sat shiplessly in the harbor.

senses.definition: a small ship or a diminutive vessel; a little ship
examples: The harbor welcomed a tiny shiplet to dock.
Historically, a shiplet served coastal routes.

senses.definition: the amount of goods or cargo that a ship can carry or has carried
examples: The shipload of cotton was worth millions.
They anticipated a full shipload for the voyage.

senses.definition: large quantities carried by ships; many loads of cargo
examples: The port received shiploads of grain.
There were shiploads of crates awaiting inspection.

senses.definition: a sailor or crew member; historically a worker aboard a ship
examples: The old shipman told tales of the sea.
Shipman duties included navigation and watch.

senses.definition: the skill of operating or handling ships; seamanship
examples: Her shipmanship improved after years at sea.
The captain praised the crew's shipmanship.

senses.definition: the vertical pole or structure on a ship that supports sails
examples: The shipmast creaked as the wind picked up.
Repairs were made to the damaged shipmast.

senses.definition: a person in charge of a ship; captain or master of a vessel
examples: The shipmaster commanded the voyage with calm precision.
In the past, a shipmaster made all navigation decisions.

senses.definition: plural of shipmaster; multiple captains or masters of ships
examples: The conference gathered several shipmasters.
Two shipmasters shared their routes.

senses.definition: a fellow crew member on a ship; a companion sailor
examples: She spoke with her shipmate during the night watch.
The shipmate shared rations with him.

senses.definition: the crew members on a ship; fellow sailors
examples: The shipmates shared a bunk they'd reserved.
All shipmates supported the captain during the voyage.

senses.definition: resembling or pertaining to shipmats; likely archaic or rare
examples: The term shipmatish appeared in old shipbuilding manuals.

senses.definition: plural of shipman; sailors or crew members
examples: The shipmen repaired the rigging.
The shipmen gathered on deck at dawn.

senses.definition: a large quantity of goods sent or transported, often by ship or other means
examples: The shipment arrived today.
They inspected the shipment for damage.

senses.definition: multiple consignments of goods sent or transported
examples: Several shipments were delayed.
Cargo shipments increased during the quarter.

senses.definition: a person or company that owns a ship
examples: The shipowner financed the new vessel.
Shipowners must comply with maritime regulations.

senses.definition: plural of shipowner; people or companies that own ships
examples: Shipowners gathered for the conference.
Many shipowners invest in greener fuels.

senses.definition: the act or business of owning ships; the enterprise of operating ships
examples: Shipowning requires capital and regulatory knowledge.
They discussed shipowning strategies.

senses.definition: able to be shipped; suitable for transportation
examples: The goods are shippable by air.
Not all items are shippable to that country.

senses.definition: the process or act of shipping goods; the act of dispatching items by transport
examples: The company tracks every shipment and shippage.
Documentation is required for accurate shippage.

senses.definition: past tense of ship; to transport something by ship or other means
examples: The goods were shipped yesterday.
We shipped the package via express air.
